question: Particle 1 starts from A with u=10 m/s, a=2 m/s^2. Exactly 5 s later Particle 2 starts from the same point A with u=20 m/s, a=1 m/s^2, same direction. Find the time (from Particle 1's start) at which Particle 2 first catches Particle 1.
answer: (D) The second particle never catches the first (discriminant < 0)

question: A stone is dropped from rest from the top of a 45 m tall building. Taking g = 10 m/s^2, how long does it take to reach the ground?

Two isosceles prisms 1 and 2 (angles A1,A2; indices n1,n2) have inner faces a1b1, a2b2 parallel and both perpendicular to a horizontal mirror M. A ray enters a1c1, leaves prism 1 at a1b1, reflects off M, enters prism 2 at a2b2, emerges from a2c2. Pick correct statement(s). Key link: i2 = e1.
options: (A) both at min deviation => n2/n1 = sin(A1/2)/sin(A2/2)  (B) sin i1 = n2 sin(A2/2)  (C) theta = (1/2)[dm1/(n1-1)+dm2/(n2-1)]  (D) if prism1 at min deviation, sin i2 = n1 sin(A1/2)

question: Charge q=1uC, mass m=1mg, v0=(i+2j) m/s at origin (y0=0). For 0<=t<0.2s: E=1i V/m with gravity -10j. At t=0.2s E off, B=6j T on. q/m=1. Phase1: a=(1i-10j); at 0.2s v=(1.2,0,0), y=0.2m. Phase2: y free-falls y=0.2-5t'^2, xz circle R=v_perp/omega=1.2/6=0.2m, omega=6. Pick correct statement(s).
options: (A) at t=0.3s, y=15 cm  (B) at t=0.4s, distance from XZ plane =10 cm  (C) radius = 20 cm  (D) returns to XZ plane at t=0.35 s

question: Charge Q1=q at P1(a,b) and Q2=mq at P2(ma,mb), with a,b nonzero and m not 0 or 1. Find the locus of points where |V1|=|V2|. Master equation: (m+1)(x^2+y^2) - 2m(ax+by) = 0. m=-1 gives a line ax+by=0; else a circle center (ma/(m+1), mb/(m+1)), radius |m|/|m+1| * sqrt(a^2+b^2).
options: (A) m=-1 => line ax+by=0  (B) m=2 => circle center (2a/3,2b/3) radius (2/3)sqrt(a^2+b^2)  (C) m=-2 => circle center (2a,2b) radius 2 sqrt(a^2+b^2)  (D) m=-3 => straight line 3bx+3ay=0

question: A particle projected from ground with speed u at angle theta explodes at the highest point into two equal masses. One fragment loses all KE and falls vertically; the other continues. Which statements are correct? At apex vx=ucosθ, vy=0, apex (R/2, H). Momentum: (M/2)V = M u cosθ => V = 2u cosθ. Time to fall t = u sinθ/g. 2nd fragment horizontal dist = R, total = 3R/2. Landing speed = u sqrt(4cos^2θ+sin^2θ). COM unaffected (internal forces).
options: (A) 2nd fragment lands at 3R/2 from projection  (B) lands with speed u sqrt(cos^2θ+4sin^2θ)  (C) COM follows original projectile trajectory  (D) time for 2nd fragment to reach ground = 2u sinθ/g

question: A ball is thrown from (0,0) with speed v0 at angle theta0 from +x. At the same time a stone is thrown from (L,0) at angle (180-theta1) with a suitable speed so as to hit the ball. For fixed v0: (theta0,theta1)=(45,45) hit after T1; (60,30) after T2. Find (T1/T2)^2. Key: equal heights need v0 sin th0 = v1 sin th1, so collision is horizontal catch-up; T = L sin th1 / (v0 sin(th0+th1)). Both cases th0+th1=90 so T ∝ sin th1: T1/T2 = sin45/sin30 = sqrt2.

10 mol ideal monoatomic gas, state a at atmospheric P, Ta=300K, volume V0. Suddenly compressed to V0/3 = state b (adiabatic). Piston fixed, submerged in 11C=284K bath -> state c (isochoric). Piston slowly back to V0 in the bath -> state f (isothermal 284K). 9^(1/3)=2.08, gamma=5/3. (A) PV schematic adiabatic a->b, isochoric b->c, isothermal c->f. (B) dU(a->b)=4860R. (C) net dU=-240R. (D) Pb=2.08 atm & Tb=624K.
options: (A) correct PV schematic  (B) dU(a->b)=4860R  (C) net dU=-240R  (D) Pb=2.08 atm & Tb=624K
answer: A, B and C (D wrong: Pb=6.24 atm)

question: JEE Advanced 2025 P2 · Monatomic gas, adiabatic+isobaric V-T cycle WXYZ (V=64,125,250). Heat absorbed along X->Y, given nRT_W=1 J.
JEE Advanced 2025 P2 · Thermally-conducting spring-loaded piston between 3/2 mol and 1 mol; piston L/2 from each edge, spring natural length 2L/5. Right pressure P=(kL/A)alpha, find alpha.
JEE Advanced 2025 P1 · Black-body plates P,Q net flux W0; insert 2 plates (3 gaps in series), steady flux Ws. Find W0/Ws.
JEE Advanced 2024 P2 · Soap bubble in air chamber, dP=4S/r=144 Pa; chamber P0 reduced to 8P0/27 isothermally. Find new excess pressure.
JEE Advanced 2023 P2 · n degrees of freedom: U_n=(n/2)RT, v=sqrt(gamma RT/M), gamma=1+2/n. Which is correct? (A) v3<v6,U3>U6 (B) v5>v3,U3>U5 (C) v5>v7,U5<U6 (D) v6<v7,U6<U7
JEE Advanced 2023 P1 · Adiabatic (T_A,V0)->(T_f,5V0) vs isothermal (T_B,V0)->(T_f,5V0). Find T_A/T_B. (A) 5^(g-1) (B) 5^(1-g) (C) 5^g (D) 5^(1+g)
JEE Advanced 2023 P1 · Match black-body T (P 2000,Q 3000,R 5000,S 10000 K) to List-II via Wien/Stefan/photoemission. (Wien b=2.9e-3, hc/e=1.24e-6).
JEE Advanced 2022 P2 · 5 mol monatomic + 1 mol rigid diatomic, adiabatically compressed V0->V0/4 (2^1.2=2.3, 2^3.2=9.2). (A) |W|=13RT0 (B) avg KE 18-19RT0 (C) Pf between 9-10 P0 (D) gamma=1.6
JEE Advanced 2020 P1 · 1 mol He isothermal V1->4V1 then adiabatic ->32V1. W_iso/W_adia=f*ln2. Find f.
JEE Advanced 2018 P2 · 1 mol monatomic adiabatic expansion V->8V, T_i=100 K, R=8.0. Find decrease in internal energy.
